page: Google Cloud Platform e2-highcpu-2 is an entry-level, compute-focused instance within the E2 machine family. It features 2 vCPUs mapped to 1 physical core with 2 threads per core, utilizing an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor running at 2.2 GHz. The instance is configured with 2.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 1.0 GB per core ratio. It does not include bundled local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Performance benchmarks position this instance in the lower tiers. Single-core stress-ng performance is weak, scoring 1105.98, while multi-core performance registers at 1452.89, placing it in the bottom 10% of tested systems. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with read-only large block speeds at 13.03 GB/sec and write speeds at 11.11 GB/sec. Cryptographic and compression tasks show similar entry-level performance, with a single-threaded gzip compression speed of 18.85 GB/sec and a Passmark CPU encryption score of 653.65.

From a resource density perspective, the e2-highcpu-2 offers a lean hardware profile designed for cost-conscious deployments. The dedicated CPU allocation provides predictable execution compared to shared-core alternatives, though the absolute compute and memory capacity limits its utility for resource-intensive applications. It is best suited for lightweight workloads such as basic static web serving, minor Redis operations, small-scale utility scripting, and development or testing environments that do not require substantial memory footprints.
description: Google Cloud Platform e2-highcpu-2 is an entry-level, compute-focused instance featuring 2 vCPUs and 2.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor running at 2.2 GHz, this instance provides a dedicated CPU allocation with 1 physical core and 2 threads. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark results indicate modest performance, with multi-core stress-ng and memory bandwidth metrics falling into the bottom 10% of tested hardware. The instance achieves a static web serving throughput of 1.39 GB/sec and a Redis SET performance of 1.34 million operations per second. Given its low memory-to-CPU ratio and entry-level performance profile, the e2-highcpu-2 is qualitatively cost-efficient for lightweight, non-memory-intensive workloads. It is best suited for basic web serving, small utility scripts, and development environments.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform e2-highcpu-2 is an entry-level instance with 2 vCPUs, 2.0 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, designed for lightweight web serving and basic development tasks.

tagline: An entry-level, compute-focused instance with dedicated vCPUs for lightweight utility workloads and basic web serving.
